'''Nightmare demon''' was the name of a near-mythical [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Droxine]]. They were small-sized [[Primate/Legends|primates]] with two tails, suction cup-tipped fingers and a bare head that resembled the [[Human/Legends|Human]] skull. Nightmare demons had strong [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] abilities that allowed them to drive other beings mad with nightmarish hallucinations. While they were believed to have been [[Speciecide|exterminated]] some [[Time/Legends|time]] before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], some nightmare demons had survived the slaughter and fallen under [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] control. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], a surviving demon named [[Reist]] [[Skirmish on Lapez 3|attempted]] to murder the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] hero [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], only to be slain himself.

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Nightmare_monkey.png|thumb|left|The nightmare demons were small-sized primates with a bare skull]]
Nightmare demons were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of small [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Primate/Legends|primates]],<ref name="CSWE" /> thus belonging to the same order of [[Mammal/Legends|mammals]]<ref name="NEGAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref> as [[Human/Legends|Humans]].<ref name="Blog">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-the-celestials|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut — The Celestials|int=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut}}</ref> As such, they were [[Humanoid/Legends|anthropoid]] in shape, characterized by a torso with four [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] and a single head attached to it. They had a [[Hair/Legends|fur]]-covered body and two tails. Their arms ended with four-fingered hands, while their feet only had three toes. Those fingers and toes were all very flexible and ended with suction cups. The nightmare demons' cranium, unlike the rest of their body, was hairless, exposing their apparent [[exoskeleton]]. Their skull looked very similar to that of the Humans, only much smaller, and their deep-set [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] flashed with eerie bursts of light. They were small enough to fit on a Human's forearm without discomfort.<ref name="dm" />

In addition to being supple and nimble, the nightmare demons demonstrated powerful [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] abilities. They were able to enter a being's mind and immobilize them with their worst fears—hence their name. However, their telepathy had its limits, as it could be blocked by [[Lead/Legends|lead]].<ref name="dm" />

==History==
{{Quote|No… It can't be… I thought they'd all been eradicted before the Clone Wars…|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="dm" />}}
[[File:Esstran_sector.jpg|thumb|right|225px|The Esstran sector, home of the demons]]
Nightmare demons evolved on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Droxine]],<ref name="dm" /> located within the [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Although not part of it, their home planet was close to the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]], the crib of the dreaded [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] civilization.<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Between [[3959 BBY|3959]] and [[3956 BBY]], the demons' home planet was part of the territory assimilated by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The nightmare demons were thought to have been completely [[Speciecide|wiped out]] at some point before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="dm" /> a pan-galactic conflict that raged from [[22 BBY/Legends|22]] to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] and ended with the [[Declaration of a New Order|proclamation]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> However, a few nightmare demons did survive the slaughter and went on to live during the ensuing [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]].<ref name="dm" />

==Nightmare demons in the galaxy==
[[File:ReistBubble.jpg|thumb|left|The nightmare demon Reist partnered with Altin Wuho]]
The nightmare demons who had survived their speciecide were eventually recovered by the Empire, who partenered them with high-level [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] chosen by [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] himself. Those who were tasked with exploiting the demons' skills would make them wear a lead-lined hood, just to shield themselves from a potentially treacherous attempt. Because of the terrible mental powers they wielded, members of other species often displayed hate and fear when meeting a nightmare demon, and the creature, in return, could feel those ill emotions.<ref name="dm" />

One of the surviving demons, a male named [[Reist]], was paired up with [[Altin Wuho]], a [[Human replica droid]] who was, by his very nature, immune to Reist's telepathic powers. Around [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], shortly after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], Wuho [[Skirmish on Lapez 3|unleashed]] his nightmare demon partner on [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], a hero and ace [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. After experiencing a series of hope-draining visions, Skywalker was able to use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to overcome the powers of Reist. Reaching his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], the pilot struck the demon in the [[Breast/Legends|chest]], mortally wounding him.<ref name="dm" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Fans missed Luke battling Reist, 'a telepathic killer from the planet Droxine.' Sounds scary, but Reist was a monkey with a skull for a head.|Jason Fry, in ''Comic Marvels''|<ref name="Comic Marvels">{{InsiderCite|91|Comic Marvels}}</ref>}}
The nightmare demons were created by [[Steve Moore]] and first appeared in his [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] ''[[the Empire Strikes Back Monthly 149]]: Death Masque'', published in [[1981]].
